_info: Founding Era — early decisions that shape the village's identity. Each event needs exactly 2 choices with label, effect, log_text, and follow_text.

village_founding.title: The Village Awakens

choices.label: Build a communal storehouse — for everyone
choices.log_text: A communal storehouse was built as the first structure.
choices.follow_text: The beams rise quickly — every hand helps. In the evenings, people gather around the half-built frame and share stories of the old lands. Something is being made here.

choices.label: Secure the village borders first — we protect our own
choices.log_text: The village borders were secured first — a palisade and watch posts.
choices.follow_text: Fences and watch posts appear at the village edge. The work takes two weeks. When it is done, the village feels smaller inside — but safer.

first_winter.title: The First Winter
first_winter.text:
Winter arrives earlier than expected. Provisions are running low.
The council gathers — what does Chieftain {chieftain} decide?

choices.label: Share equally — no one is left behind (+5 alignment)
choices.log_text: All shared equally — the community survived the winter.
choices.follow_text: Winter passes, and everyone has survived. Leaner than hoped — but no empty place by the fire.

choices.label: The strong eat first — the weak must wait (-10 alignment)
choices.log_text: The strong were favoured. Some of the weak did not survive the winter.
choices.follow_text: The children of the weak watch with wide eyes at the tables of the strong. Some will never forget this winter.

trade_caravan.title: Travelling Merchants
trade_caravan.text:
Merchants from the south are resting at the village edge.
They offer bronze tools in exchange for food stores.
What does {chieftain} decide?

choices.label: Negotiate fairly — a just exchange (+5 alignment)
choices.log_text: A fair exchange was agreed. The merchants will return.
choices.follow_text: The merchants laugh as they depart and promise to return. Your name travels well along the trade roads.

choices.label: Take advantage — the strangers don't know the true value (-10 alignment)
choices.log_text: The ignorance of the strangers was exploited. Word got around.
choices.follow_text: The merchants leave without smiling. Weeks later a rumour drifts back — they call your village 'the Greedy Ones'.

border_dispute.title: The Boundary Stones
border_dispute.text:
A neighbouring clan has moved their boundary stones overnight, claiming a stretch of your best farmland.
The council looks to {chieftain} for a response.

choices.label: Seek mediation — send envoys and negotiate (+5 alignment)
choices.log_text: A peaceful mediation was sought with the neighbouring clan.
choices.follow_text: After tense talks, a new line is drawn that both sides accept — barely. The farmland is split, but blood was not spilled.

choices.label: Move the stones back by night and double the watch (-5 alignment)
choices.log_text: The boundary stones were moved back under cover of darkness.
choices.follow_text: The land is yours again, for now. But the neighbours haven't forgotten — and neither have your sentries, who scan the dark every night.

wandering_healer.title: The Wandering Healer
wandering_healer.text:
A lone healer arrives at the village gate, offering their knowledge of herbs and remedies in exchange for shelter and a place among your people.
The council debates.

choices.label: Welcome them openly — knowledge is worth more than bread (+10 alignment)
choices.log_text: The wandering healer was welcomed into the community.
choices.follow_text: The healer settles in quietly and is soon known to all. Children follow them into the forest to learn the names of plants. The village feels a little less alone.

choices.label: Turn them away — we cannot feed another mouth (-5 alignment)
choices.log_text: The wandering healer was turned away from the village.
choices.follow_text: The healer nods without a word and walks on down the road. That winter, three people fall ill with a fever no one knows how to treat.

smoke_on_the_horizon.title: Smoke on the Horizon
smoke_on_the_horizon.text:
Scouts return at dusk with troubled faces. Tracks in the eastern forest — unfamiliar boots, a cold fire pit, the smell of old smoke. Someone has been watching your settlement for days.

The council gathers. No one sleeps well. {chieftain} must decide before dawn.

choices.label: Send rangers — track them, show this land has teeth (-5 alignment)
choices.log_text: Rangers were sent into the forest to track the strangers.
choices.follow_text: The rangers return two days later, hollow-eyed. They found the camp — abandoned in haste, bones of a deer still warm. Whoever watched your village has moved on. For now. The rangers say nothing of what else they found in that camp. Some things are better left in the dark.

choices.label: Set the men to building — palisade, watchtower, a ditch
choices.log_text: The settlement turned inward — palisade and watchtower raised.
choices.follow_text: Week after week the posts go in, the ditches deepen, the watchtower grows. The strangers never come. Maybe they saw the walls rising and decided to look elsewhere. Maybe they are still out there, patient. Either way, your people sleep better — and that counts for something.

choices.label: Send an envoy with bread and salt — meet them as neighbours (+10 alignment)
choices.log_text: An envoy was sent into the forest with food and an offer of peace.
choices.follow_text: The envoy returns the next evening with a story: a band of thirty, driven from their valley by a chieftain who took everything. They carry little but hunger and children. {chieftain} opens the gate. Some in the council grumble. The strangers become neighbours — quietly, one shared fire at a time.

storehouse_test._info: Only appears if has_storehouse flag is set. The storehouse built earlier becomes relevant.
storehouse_test.title: The Empty Storehouse
storehouse_test.text:
A bad harvest. The storehouse that the village built in its first season stands half-empty by midwinter.

Because it exists, the rationing is organised. Because it exists, {chieftain} can see exactly how bad things are — and act before people go hungry.

What is the priority now?

choices.label: Open the stores fully — no one goes hungry this winter (+10 alignment, -10 population long-term risk)
choices.log_text: The storehouse was opened fully during the hard winter.
choices.follow_text: The stores run out before spring. It was the right thing to do — and now there is nothing left. Next autumn, the village will need to be more careful.

choices.label: Ration strictly — protect the seed grain above all
choices.log_text: Strict rationing was enforced to protect the seed grain.
choices.follow_text: Some go to bed hungry. The seed grain survives intact. In spring, the fields are full — and people remember both things.

palisade_test._info: Only appears if has_palisade flag is set. A threat arrives and the palisade matters.
palisade_test.title: The Night Watch
palisade_test.text:
Fires on the ridge to the east — too many, too close. Someone is watching the settlement.

The watch post your people built in the founding year sees them first. There is time to prepare.

Without it, there would not have been.

choices.label: Stand at the gate and show force — let them see we are ready
choices.log_text: Armed villagers stood at the gate to meet the threat.
choices.follow_text: By morning the fires are gone. No one came. Whether they saw the palisade and thought better of it — nobody knows. The watch stays doubled for a week.

choices.label: Send a runner to the neighbours — warn them, stand together (+10 alignment)
choices.log_text: Neighbouring settlements were warned and a joint watch organised.
choices.follow_text: The messenger returns with six armed men from the next valley. The fires fade. In the morning a neighbour's elder clasps {chieftain}'s arm. 'We owe you one,' he says.

drought_warning.title: The Dry Summer
drought_warning.text:
The river runs lower than anyone can remember. The elders speak of drought.
Before the wells run dry, {chieftain} must act.

choices.label: Ration water now and dig a deeper well together
choices.log_text: Water was rationed and a deeper well was dug communally.
choices.follow_text: The work is backbreaking, but the new well holds. The rationing was hard — arguments broke out over buckets — yet the village made it through.

choices.label: Divert the stream from the neighbouring farmstead — we need it more (-10 alignment)
choices.log_text: The stream was diverted from a neighbouring farmstead.
choices.follow_text: The crops are saved. The neighbour's fields wither. They come to complain, but there is nothing to discuss — the channel is already dug.
